title: nepeta brachyantha
intro: Nepeta brachyantha, also known as dwarf catmint or Nepeta, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. It belongs to the mint family and is characterized by its small, blue or purple flowers and grey-green foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as digestive problems, headaches, and respiratory issues. The leaves and flowers of Nepeta brachyantha can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
